commit.message:
fix: resolve runner startup issue with explicit entrypoint

Runner v2.332.0 no longer auto-invokes run.sh from the default
ENTRYPOINT when used with ARC JIT config injection. Fix by explicitly
setting command + args and redirecting stderr to stdout for log
visibility.

Also add RBAC Role/RoleBinding for arc-runner service account
(needed if containerMode: kubernetes is re-enabled later).

[feature] DB-first for fulltext, embeddings, and deep read claims

- batch_embed_papers: check central DB for cached embeddings before
  calling Gemini API (2301 embeddings available)

fix: validate DefaultFrom as a valid RFC 2822 email address (#163)

Add net/mail.ParseAddress() validation in config validate() so that a
non-empty DefaultFrom is rejected unless it is a valid RFC 2822 address
(plain or display-name form). Change the frontend input from type="email"
to type="text" and update the placeholder so users can enter
"Name <email>" without the browser blocking submission.

fix: parse JSONB links safely and handle null onboarding_completed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SSN auto-populate, Stripe checkout user ID, and Stripe error visibility

SSN fix:
- getUserProfile() in ClaimBuilder now reads SSN from sessionStorage("sessionSSN")
  alongside localStorage profile. SSN was correctly stored in sessionStorage on the
  Profile page but never read back in ClaimBuilder, so printed statements showed
  placeholder text instead of the veteran's SSN.

Stripe checkout fix:
- Use effectiveUserId (real DB row id from ensureUserRow) instead of user.id for
  storage.updateUser() and stripeService.createCustomer(). Previously the Insforge
  session id was used, which may not match the actual users table row id (stale data
  scenario), causing updateUser to throw a RLS/not-found error mid-checkout.
- Also use service-level client (no accessToken) for the updateUser call so RLS
  does not block saving the Stripe customer id back to the users row.

Error visibility fix:
- safeErrorMessage() now surfaces Stripe-specific errors in production (e.g.
  "No such price", "Invalid API key") so operators/users can diagnose payment
  config issues instead of seeing only the generic fallback message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Major architecture revision — domain agents as doorkeepers, unified heap

Core changes:
- No classifier step. Domain agents self-select relevance from the context blob.
- Context blob contains ALL agents with relevance scores. Data integrity over context management.
- Unified max-heap for all work. Three inference types: estimation (very high), job (high), batch (very low).
- Estimation call outputs complexity score in tool calls. Complexity ≤1 executes inline immediately.
- Internal Router simplified — drops off turn, routes to agents. Job formation moves to domain agent.
- Estimator reads complexity score, not a complex job_envelope_v1.
- Preemptive scheduling — higher priority work interrupts in-flight jobs at tool call boundaries. Context serialized to FIFO stack for resumption.
- Batch processing at night — weight updates deferred from real-time. RDS tracks routing for batch.
- Working state vs committed state — system writes freely to scratch space, commits require user approval.

New doc: docs/arch/v1/scheduler.md
Updated: context-engine.md, task-formation.md, executor.md, next-steps.md, dataflow-walk-notes.md
Updated: skyra/internal/agent/README.md, skyra/internal/delegation/README.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add simulation framework with parallel test execution (#9)

## Summary

- **`loom sim` command**: Discover and run HDL testbenches through
simulator backends with compile → elaborate → simulate pipeline
- **Parallel execution (`-j N`)**: Run up to N tests concurrently using
`std::thread::scope` with counting semaphore — per-test build
directories prevent file conflicts
- **Windows argument quoting fix**: New `tool_arg()` utility handles
`cmd.exe` mangling of `=` in defines (e.g., `-d SIM=1`) across all 7
simulator backends
- **Shell completions & watch mode**: New `loom completions` subcommand
and `loom watch --sim` for auto-rerun on file changes
- **sim_demo example**: ALU + FIFO testbenches demonstrating test
discovery, suites, tags, and compatibility filtering

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Gaia NSS catalog integration for starting points and comparison plotting

Adds functions to query the Gaia Non-Single Star (NSS) two-body orbit
catalog and use published orbital solutions to initialize Octofitter
models and compare posteriors:

- query_nss(): query NSS table by Gaia source ID (DR3/DR4)
- nss_to_starting_point(): convert NSS solution to initialize! params,
  auto-detecting Thiele-Innes vs Campbell vs UniformCircular parameterizations
- initialize_from_nss!(): one-liner to query + initialize from NSS
- nss_to_model_chain(): build a model+chain from NSS for overlay plotting
  with PairPlots/octoplot (mass and parallax derived automatically)

NSS solutions are used as starting points only (not priors) since their
uncertainties may be overly optimistic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add 431 patch files and postinstall system for upstream modifications

Phase 3: Generate patch files for every upstream React Native file that
has macOS-specific modifications (#if TARGET_OS_OSX guards, // [macOS]
markers, Platform.OS === 'macos' checks).

Breakdown by type:
- 361 patch files (small surgical changes)
- 70 derived files (heavily modified)
- 27 platform files (new macOS-only files in upstream dirs)

The postinstall script automatically applies these patches to the local
react-native installation in node_modules when the package is installed.

Top modified files by scope:
- RCTViewComponentView.mm (+856/-361)
- RCTView.m (+811/-47)
- RCTConvert.mm (+460/-41)
- RCTUITextField.mm (+443/-6)
- RCTBaseTextInputView.mm (+397/-33)
- RCTScrollView.m (+395/-33)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Deduplicate contacts when creating a campaign

Contacts with multiple tags could appear in the contactIds array more
than once when multiple tags are selected. Switch from nested create to
createMany with skipDuplicates and deduplicate the IDs server-side so
each contact is only added to the campaign once.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stableId missing after login — auto-link owned stable

AuthContext:
- After fetching profile, if stable_id is null, check stables
  table for a stable owned by this user (owner_user_id = userId)
- If found, update profile with stable_id and use it
- Fetch stable_name for display in UI

Hooks (useHorses, useClients, useStalls, useTasks):
- Add explicit check: if logged in but stableId missing, log
  warning and show empty state (not mock data)
- Helps debug why data is empty

Fix bcrypt/passlib incompatibility on Python 3.13

Replace passlib with direct bcrypt calls — passlib's detect_wrap_bug
crashes with bcrypt 4.x on Python 3.13. Remove passlib dependency.

commit.message:
feat: Auto-discover plugins for bootstrap (schema v4)

Replace opt-in enabled_plugins config with auto-discovery: all installed
plugins with a bootstrap.json are bootstrapped automatically. bootstrap_cache
tracks confirmed plugins to avoid repeated filesystem scans; no_bootstrap
allows permanent opt-out.

- plugin_resolve: rewrite list_enabled_plugins to scan registry, cache hits,
  purge stale entries; returns (results, cache_changed) tuple
- bootstrap_engine: unpack tuple, persist config when cache changes
- config: bump schema to v4, migrate v3→v4 (remove enabled_plugins,
  add no_bootstrap/bootstrap_cache)
- defaults/config.json: update to v4 schema
- ARCHITECTURE.md: document auto-discovery + cache behavior
- tests: update migration tests for v4, fix git dep test (use unreachable URL),
  delete test_has_required_tools (unreal-kit no longer declares tools)

commit.message:
fix: past months correctly reflect settlements made in later months

Settlements were time-filtered to the selected month, so viewing January
after clearing the remaining debt in February still showed an outstanding
balance. Settlements should never be time-filtered: they reduce the total
outstanding balance regardless of when they were recorded. Only expenses
remain filtered through the selected month for carryover tracking.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 1pass plugin for op CLI and op-exec management (#215)

* feat: add 1password-tool plugin with op and op-exec support

Adds a new plugin for 1Password CLI (op) and op-exec (nsheaps/op-exec):

- SessionStart hook for auto-installing op and op-exec in web sessions
- Skills for both op CLI and op-exec usage
- Default auto_install set to false (prefer mise-managed installation)
- mise.toml updated with ubi:1Password/cli (op-exec commented out
  pending GitHub releases availability)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SxTL3dgvyj81ARAA95pzh1

* fix: use aqua backend for 1Password CLI in mise.toml

ubi:1Password/cli returns 404 because 1Password doesn't publish
GitHub releases. Switch to aqua:1password/cli which uses the
aqua registry and knows how to download from 1Password's CDN.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SxTL3dgvyj81ARAA95pzh1

* fix: use vfox backend for 1Password CLI in mise

aqua:1password/cli fails with "latest" because the 1Password CDN
URL template embeds the version string literally and doesn't resolve
"latest". Switch to vfox:mise-plugins/vfox-1password which has proper
version resolution logic.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SxTL3dgvyj81ARAA95pzh1

* chore: `just lint`

* chore: rename plugin from 1password-tool to 1pass

Renames the plugin directory and all internal references
(plugin.json name, settings key, PLUGIN_NAME, skills, README).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SxTL3dgvyj81ARAA95pzh1

* fix: update marketplace.json with missing plugins

The CD workflow has been failing to push marketplace updates to main
due to branch protection rules requiring changes through PRs (GH013).

This adds 5 plugins that were missing from marketplace.json:
- 1pass (new in this PR)
- mise (renamed from mise-tool)
- gh-tool
- permissions-sync
- sequential-thinking

Without marketplace entries, Claude Code cannot resolve these plugins
from the nsheaps-claude-plugins marketplace, so their SessionStart
hooks (like mise auto-install) never fire.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SxTL3dgvyj81ARAA95pzh1

* fix: correct version resolution repo and mise backend reference

- install-op.sh: use 1Password/cli (op CLI) instead of 1Password/connect
  (Connect server) for tool_resolve_github_version — these are different
  products with different version numbers
- SKILL.md: update mise backend from ubi:1Password/cli to
  vfox:mise-plugins/vfox-1password to match mise.toml

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SxTL3dgvyj81ARAA95pzh1

* revert: remove marketplace.json changes (blocked by CI)

The check-version-files CI job blocks marketplace.json modifications
in PRs — it's auto-generated by CD on merge. The CD push failure
(#217) needs to be fixed first, then the marketplace will update
automatically when this PR merges.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SxTL3dgvyj81ARAA95pzh1

* feat: enable op-exec via mise now that GitHub releases are available

op-exec v0.0.1 is now published on GitHub with platform binaries.
Uncommented the mise.toml entry and updated from deprecated ubi:
backend to github: backend. Updated README and SKILL.md references.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SxTL3dgvyj81ARAA95pzh1

* fix: use 1Password update endpoint instead of nonexistent GitHub repo

1Password/cli is not a public GitHub repo — tool_resolve_github_version
always returned the hardcoded fallback, risking downgrades when the
installed version was newer than the fallback.

Replace with resolve_latest_op_version that queries 1Password's own
update endpoint (app-updates.agilebits.com) to get the actual latest
CLI version. Also fix op-exec fallback from 0.1.0 to 0.0.1 (actual
release version).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SxTL3dgvyj81ARAA95pzh1

* feat: add multiplatform support for op and op-exec downloads

Detect OS (linux/darwin) and architecture (amd64/arm64) at runtime
instead of hardcoding linux_amd64. Both download_op and download_op_exec
now use the detected platform for their download URLs.

Supported platforms:
- op CLI: linux/darwin x amd64/arm64
- op-exec: linux/darwin x amd64/arm64

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SxTL3dgvyj81ARAA95pzh1

* feat: video pipeline §15 multi-channel architecture + spam avoidance

Add §15 (multi-channel architecture and platform spam avoidance) to
VIDEO_PIPELINE_SPEC.md covering:
- 24-channel architecture: each channel gets its own image bank,
  visual style, TTS voice, assembly profile, metadata template,
  upload timing window, and background music pool
- Image bank sizing: ~400 per channel, 15 topics × 24-27 images;
  9,600 total across 24 channels
- 6 style families mapped to channel groups 1-24
- Assembly differentiation: 5 dimensions, ≥3 must differ per channel
- 6 hard platform spam avoidance rules (YouTube/IG/TikTok):
  no cross-channel asset sharing, staggered upload windows,
  unique TTS voices, metadata template diversity, sequence
  signature dedup, 3-video/day upload cap at launch
- channel_registry.yaml schema spec (new config file)
- Updated image bank size note to 400/channel + 9,600 total

channel_registry.yaml: all 24 channels with isolated image banks mapped to:

  - Brand alignment: stillness_press (emotional/healing) vs

  - override_shared_negatives flag (anime channels override "no anime style")
  - Assembly profile per channel (pacing, transition, caption, aspect)

image_prompt_templates.yaml: 3-part Flux T2I prompt templates per style:
  - foreground: style + subject + palette + composition
  - background: atmospheric gradient per style
  - lighting: lighting + mood + "9:16, highly detailed but soft"
  - negative_overrides: style-specific negatives
  - topic_subject_hints: 4 topic examples per style as starting points
  All 24 styles fully specified with style_modifier tokens.

commit.message:
fix: align dataset generator dealer derivation and strategy lifecycle with engine (#565)

* fix: align dealer derivation and strategy lifecycle with engine

Two correctness fixes from review:

1. _deterministic_dealer() used _deal_rng formula (seed * 1_000_003 + deal_id)
   but the real engine uses random.Random(seed + deal_id) for dealer in
   auction mode. Fixed to match play_single_hand() exactly. Added
   test_matches_engine_formula to lock alignment.

2. _play_tricks() did not call on_hand_start() or observe_play() on
   GluttonStrategy. The strategy uses these to track seen cards, infer
   voids, and set contract/trump context. Without them, suit and low
   contract rollouts diverge from actual gameplay. Fixed to match the
   engine's strategy lifecycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Optimize 3D animation: remove O(n²) stress calc, add bulk position API

The solver is O(n²) per iteration — that's inherent to stress majorization.
But we were adding an unnecessary O(n²) compute_stress call every frame
just to populate a field we never display.

- stepOnce(): run one iteration without building a result object
- getPositions(): bulk [x,y,z,...] export, no bounding box allocation
- build_result: skip stress computation unless explicitly requested
- JS: skip edge wireframe rebuild during animation (show on converge)
